left-right-join.label: LEFT and RIGHT JOIN
left-right-join.summary: Include unmatched rows with LEFT and RIGHT JOIN.

sketch0.title: What are LEFT and RIGHT JOINs?

sketch1.title: Why use LEFT or RIGHT JOIN?

sketch-what-is-left-right-join.title: What are LEFT and RIGHT JOINs?
sketch-what-is-left-right-join.bodyMarkdown:
A **LEFT JOIN** returns all rows from the left table and the matched rows from the right table. If there is no match, the result is NULL on the right side. A **RIGHT JOIN** does the opposite: it returns all rows from the right table and the matched rows from the left table, filling in NULLs when there is no match.

For example, if you want to list all students and any enrollments they have, you can use a LEFT JOIN between `students` and `enrollments`:

```sql
SELECT students.name, enrollments.course_id
FROM students
LEFT JOIN enrollments ON students.id = enrollments.student_id;
```

This query lists every student, even those who are not enrolled in any course (their `course_id` will be NULL).

sketch-why-use-left-right-join.title: Why use LEFT or RIGHT JOIN?
sketch-why-use-left-right-join.bodyMarkdown:
LEFT and RIGHT JOINs are useful when you want to include all records from one table, even if there are no matching records in the other table. For example, to find students who are not enrolled in any courses, you can use a LEFT JOIN and look for rows where the joined table's columns are NULL.

Similarly, if you want to see all courses and which students (if any) are enrolled, you can use a RIGHT JOIN (or a LEFT JOIN with the tables swapped).

sketch-visual-example.title: Visual Example
sketch-visual-example.bodyMarkdown:
| students.id | students.name | enrollments.course_id |
|-------------|--------------|----------------------|
| 1           | Mia          | 1                    |
| 1           | Mia          | 2                    |
| 2           | Leo          | 2                    |
| 2           | Leo          | 3                    |
| 3           | Ava          | 4                    |
| 4           | Noah         | 1                    |
| 4           | Noah         | 4                    |
| 5           | Emma         | 3                    |
| 6           | Liam         | NULL                 |

Notice how Liam appears with NULL for `course_id` because he is not enrolled in any course. This is the effect of a LEFT JOIN.

quiz-left-join-definition.title: What does a LEFT JOIN do?
quiz-left-join-definition.prompt: Which statement best describes a LEFT JOIN?
quiz-left-join-definition.hint: Think about which table's rows are always included.

help.concept: A LEFT JOIN returns all rows from the left table, and any matching rows from the right table. If there is no match, the right table's columns are NULL.
help.hint_1: LEFT JOIN keeps all rows from the left table, even if there's no match in the right table.
help.hint_2: The left table is fully preserved; the right table only appears where there is a match.

options.a: Returns only rows that have matching values in both tables.
options.b: Returns all rows from the left table and matched rows from the right table.
options.c: Returns all rows from the right table and matched rows from the left table.
options.d: Returns only unmatched rows from both tables.

quiz-find-students-no-enrollments.title: Finding students with no enrollments
quiz-find-students-no-enrollments.prompt: You want to find students who are not enrolled in any courses. Which of the following approaches would work? (Select all that apply.)
quiz-find-students-no-enrollments.hint: Think about which join includes all students, even those without enrollments.

help.concept: To find students with no enrollments, use the correct answer LEFT JOIN from students to enrollments and look for NULLs in the enrollments columns. Alternatively, the correct answer RIGHT JOIN from enrollments to students also works if you filter for NULLs in enrollments.
help.hint_1: LEFT JOIN from students to enrollments will include all students, even those with no enrollments.
help.hint_2: Look for NULLs in the enrollments columns after the correct answer LEFT JOIN, or use the correct answer RIGHT JOIN from enrollments to students and filter for NULLs in enrollments.

options.a: Use a LEFT JOIN from students to enrollments and filter where enrollments.student_id IS NULL.
options.b: Use an INNER JOIN between students and enrollments.
options.c: Use a LEFT JOIN from enrollments to students and filter where students.id IS NULL.
options.d: Use a RIGHT JOIN from enrollments to students and filter where enrollments.student_id IS NULL.

quiz-drag-reorder-left-join-steps.title: Steps to write a LEFT JOIN query
quiz-drag-reorder-left-join-steps.prompt: Put the steps to write a LEFT JOIN query in order.
quiz-drag-reorder-left-join-steps.hint: Start with SELECT, then FROM, then JOIN, then ON.

help.concept: A typical LEFT JOIN query starts with SELECT, then FROM (left table), then LEFT JOIN (right table), and finally the ON clause.
help.hint_1: SELECT comes first, then FROM, then LEFT JOIN, then ON.
help.hint_2: The order is: SELECT, FROM, LEFT JOIN, ON.

tokens.t1: Write SELECT and the columns you want.
tokens.t2: Specify the first (left) table in FROM.
tokens.t3: Add LEFT JOIN and the second (right) table.
tokens.t4: Write the ON clause to define how the tables are related.

quiz-fill-blank-choice-left-join-syntax.title: LEFT JOIN syntax
quiz-fill-blank-choice-left-join-syntax.prompt:
Fill in the blank to complete the LEFT JOIN syntax:

SELECT * FROM students ___ enrollments ON students.id = enrollments.student_id;
quiz-fill-blank-choice-left-join-syntax.hint: Which JOIN keeps all rows from the left table?

help.concept: the missing term is used to include all rows from the left table and matched rows from the right table.
help.hint_1: You want to include all students, even those with no enrollments.
help.hint_2: The correct keyword is the missing term.

quiz-fill-blank-choice-left-join-syntax.template: SELECT * FROM students [___] enrollments ON students.id = enrollments.student_id;

quiz-sql-left-join-list-all-students-and.title: SQL: List all students and their enrolled course titles (if any)
quiz-sql-left-join-list-all-students-and.prompt: Write a SQL query to list all students' names and the titles of the courses they are enrolled in. Include students who are not enrolled in any course (show NULL for course title).
quiz-sql-left-join-list-all-students-and.hint: Use LEFT JOIN from students to enrollments, then to courses.

help.concept: To include all students, use LEFT JOIN from students to enrollments, then LEFT JOIN to courses to get course titles.
help.hint_1: Start with students, LEFT JOIN enrollments on student id, then LEFT JOIN courses on course id.
help.hint_2: Select students.name and courses.title, joining through enrollments.

quiz-sql-left-join-list-all-students-and.starterCode:
-- List all students and their course titles (if any)
SELECT
